questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the legs are $3$ and $4$. The length of the hypotenuse is _____ .

figure.caption: Right triangle with legs 3 and 4 and the right angle at the bottom-left.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='40,130 160,130 40,40' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='118' width='12' height='12' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1'/><text x='95' y='145' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>4</text><text x='20' y='90' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>3</text><text x='105' y='80' font-size='12' fill='#ef4444'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pythagoras: hypotenuse $=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}$.

**Working.** $\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article costing $200$ is sold for $250$. The profit percentage is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit\% $=\dfrac{\text{SP}-\text{CP}}{\text{CP}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{250-200}{200}\times 100 = 25\%$$

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On the clock face shown, the time is $3{:}00$. Compute the smaller angle between the hour and minute hands (degrees).

figure.caption: Clock at 3:00
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='85' y2='28' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='133' y2='80' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='55' y='160' font-size='8'>time = 3:00</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark is $30^\circ$ apart.

**Working.**
$$3\text{ hours}\times30^\circ=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum divided by count.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10+20+30+40+50}{5}=\frac{150}{5}=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The largest number that divides $70$ and $125$, leaving remainders $5$ and $8$ respectively, is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Subtract the remainders, then take the HCF.

**Working.** $70-5=65$, $125-8=117$; HCF$(65,117)=13$.

**Answer.** $13$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same plus-shaped net (faces 2, 3, 4 in a row with 1 above 3 and 5, 6 below 3), which face is opposite face 3?

figure.caption: The same plus-shaped cube net with faces 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.08)'><rect x='70' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='30' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='110' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='100' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='140' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='86' y='45'>1</text><text x='46' y='85'>2</text><text x='86' y='85'>3</text><text x='126' y='85'>4</text><text x='86' y='125'>5</text><text x='86' y='165'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Along the vertical strip 1-3-5-6, face 3 has 1 and 5 as neighbours; the remaining strip face 6 ends up opposite 3.

**Working.** With 3 as front, 1 is top, 5 is bottom and 6 wraps to the back, so 3 is opposite 6.

**Answer.** 6.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The total surface area of a cube of side $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Surface area of a cube $=6\,(\text{side})^2$.

**Working.** $6\times 5^2 = 6\times 25 = 150$.

**Answer.** $150$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An arrow rotates $90^\circ$ clockwise each step: up, right, down, ... The next arrow points

figure.caption: Three arrows pointing up, right and down, followed by a question mark.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continue the $90^\circ$ clockwise rotation.

**Working.** After down, a further clockwise quarter-turn points to the left.

**Answer.** Left (west).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If today is Monday, what day of the week will it be after $15$ days?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Days repeat every $7$; take the remainder.

**Working.** $15\bmod 7 = 1$, so one day after Monday is Tuesday.

**Answer.** Tuesday.

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $18\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 21</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$18\times21=378.$$

**Answer.** $378$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste of $1000\,\text{kg}$ yields landfill gas at $0.15\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$. The total gas generated is _____ m³.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas $=$ mass $\times$ specific yield.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.15=150.$$

**Answer.** $150\,\text{m}^3$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: For the landfill shown, read the usable air-space and the annual fill rate from the diagram. Its design life is _____ years.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="100" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.45"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><text x="170" y="86" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">air-space = 3,00,000 m³</text><text x="170" y="108" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">fill rate = 50,000 m³/yr</text><text x="170" y="166" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">design life = air-space ÷ fill rate</text></svg>
figure.caption: Landfill air-space and annual fill rate marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life = air-space/annual fill, both read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{300000}{50000}=6.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=3.75\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $3.75\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $4$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$12000\times0.1^{3}=12.$$

**Answer.** $12$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Which stage of the EIA cycle immediately follows screening?

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="8" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="38" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Screening</text><path d="M68 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="78" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="108" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Scoping</text><path d="M138 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="148" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="178" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Impact</text><text x="178" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">prediction</text><path d="M208 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="218" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="248" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">EMP</text><path d="M278 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="288" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="318" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Review &</text><text x="318" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">clearance</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order: screening → scoping → impact prediction → EMP → review/clearance.

**Working.**
Scoping follows screening.

**Answer.** Scoping

questions.stem: A higher value of the Shannon–Wiener index $H'$ indicates

**Concept.** H' increases with richness and evenness.

Greater diversity.

PDCA.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Which are recognised biodiversity hotspots in/around India?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Western Ghats, Himalaya and Indo-Burma are global hotspots; the Thar is not (low endemism/richness threshold).

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Western Ghats, Himalaya, Indo-Burma

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $5\,\text{t}$ of methane (GWP$_{100}=25$). Its carbon-dioxide-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 125</text><rect x="190" y="145.2" width="46" height="4.8" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="141.2" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 5</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄ → CO₂-e (t)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Methane converted to CO₂-equivalent via GWP.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e $=$ mass $\times$ GWP.

**Working.**
$$5\times25=125.$$

**Answer.** $125\,\text{t CO}_2\text{-e}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Bacteria grow exponentially with specific growth rate $\mu=0.35\,\text{h}^{-1}$. The doubling (generation) time is _____ hours.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 180" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="158" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="158" x2="312" y2="158" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="28" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">ln X</text><text x="270" y="174" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><path d="M38 150 L80 148 L150 60 L230 40 L300 95" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.7"/><text x="50" y="142" font-size="7" fill="#1e293b">lag</text><text x="105" y="100" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">log (μ_max)</text><text x="215" y="34" font-size="7" fill="#1e293b">stationary</text><text x="278" y="110" font-size="7" fill="#dc2626">death</text></svg>
figure.caption: Batch microbial growth curve.

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $5$ identical sources each $75\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$75+10\log5=81.99.$$

**Answer.** $81.99\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 8 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 400 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times1000}{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.4 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\text{depth}\div\text{time}$, both read from the trajectory.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.4\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=6\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="120" width="50" height="30" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="116" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">400</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{400}\right)\times100=75\%.$$

**Answer.** $75\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The main purpose of maintaining ~850–1100 °C with adequate residence time in an MSW incinerator is to
options: ensure complete combustion and destroy dioxins/pathogens
reduce the need for air pollution control
questions.solution:
**Concept.** High temperature + 2 s residence ensures complete burnout and dioxin destruction.

**Working.**
Complete combustion.

**Answer.** Complete combustion

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A treatment plant receives BOD $200\,\text{mg/L}$ and discharges $30\,\text{mg/L}$. The BOD removal efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 200</text><rect x="190" y="132" width="46" height="18" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="128" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 30</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">BOD (mg/L)</text></svg>
figure.caption: BOD: influent vs. effluent (mg/L).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta=\dfrac{S_0-S_e}{S_0}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{200-30}{200}\times100=85\%.$$

**Answer.** $85\%$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=100\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 60 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 3 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times3\times30\times15}e^{-60^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00000791.$$

**Answer.** $0.079\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 350 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="40" width="110" height="70" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="115" y="78" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Aeration</text><text x="115" y="92" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">MLSS, V</text><circle cx="240" cy="75" r="34" fill="rgba(22,163,74,0.10)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="240" y="72" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Clarifier</text><path d="M20 70 l40 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="22" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Q, S0</text><path d="M170 70 l36 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M274 70 l44 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="290" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Qe, Se</text><path d="M240 109 l0 36 -180 0 0 -35" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="120" y="158" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">return sludge Qr</text><path d="M250 140 l40 0" stroke="#7c2d12" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="255" y="135" font-size="8" fill="#7c2d12">waste Qw</text></svg>
figure.caption: Activated-sludge: aeration basin, secondary clarifier, sludge recycle.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** F/M = QS₀/(VX).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10000\times200}{2500\times2500}=0.32.$$

**Answer.** $0.32$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A discrete particle ($\rho_s=2650\,\text{kg/m}^3$) settles in water ($\rho=1000$, $\mu=10^{-3}\,\text{Pa·s}$). Reading its diameter from the figure, its terminal velocity is _____ ×10⁻³ m/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="20" width="180" height="140" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.06)" stroke="#1e293b"/><circle cx="150" cy="66" r="12" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><line x1="138" y1="66" x2="162" y2="66" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/><text x="168" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">d = 0.05 mm</text><path d="M150 82 l0 52 m-4 -6 l4 6 4 -6" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="158" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">v_s</text><text x="150" y="174" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">ρ_s = 2650 kg/m³, water μ = 10⁻³ Pa·s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle diameter marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\dfrac{g(\rho_s-\rho)d^2}{18\mu}$, with $d$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{9.81\times1650\times(0.00005)^2}{18\times10^{-3}}=0.00225\,\text{m/s}.$$

**Answer.** $2.248\times10^{-3}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=7\%$, $O=35\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 17.2</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(7-35/8)=17205.$$

**Answer.** $17205\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 5</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.08)^{5}}=34029.$$

**Answer.** $₹34029$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">20</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="78" width="50" height="72" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="74" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">12</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$20\times\dfrac{50}{20}+12\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=99.4.$$

**Answer.** $99.4$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="142.5" width="46" height="7.5" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="138.5" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">5</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">2</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80+5\times28+2\times265=750.$$

**Answer.** $750$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $80\,\text{g/s}$ of a pollutant. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level concentration on the plume centreline (full ground reflection, $y=0$) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 40 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 4 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 40 m,  σ_z = 25 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $C=\dfrac{Q}{\pi u\sigma_y\sigma_z}\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{H^2}{2\sigma_z^2}\right)$ (ground, centreline, full reflection); $H,u,\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times4\times40\times25}e^{-40^2/(2\times25^2)}=0.00177004\,\text{g/m}^3.$$

**Answer.** $17.7\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $20\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">25</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times25}{20}=0.0625.$$

**Answer.** $0.0625\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">50</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">2</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">0.5</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$50+2\times28+0.5\times265=238.5.$$

**Answer.** $238.5$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $3$ identical sources each $80\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log3=84.77.$$

**Answer.** $84.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $8\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times200/8})\times100=95.02\%.$$

**Answer.** $95.02\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $10\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 3</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.1)^{3}}=37566.$$

**Answer.** $₹37566$

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 40</text></svg>

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: For an ideal gas at 25 °C and 1 atm, converting a gaseous pollutant from ppm(v) to mg/m³ requires multiplying by
questions.solution:
**Concept.** mg/m³ = ppm × MW / 24.45 (molar volume 24.45 L at 25°C, 1 atm).

**Working.**
× MW/24.45.

**Answer.** MW/24.45

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Anaerobic digestion of $1000\,\text{kg}$ of volatile solids yields biogas at $0.4\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg VS}$, of which $60\%$ is methane (calorific value $36\,\text{MJ/m}^3$). The recoverable energy is _____ MJ.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Energy $=$ VS $\times$ gas yield $\times$ CH₄ fraction $\times$ calorific value.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.4\times0.6\times36=8640\,\text{MJ}.$$

**Answer.** $8640\,\text{MJ}$

**Answer.** $14544\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $20\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">10</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times10}{20}=0.025.$$

**Answer.** $0.025\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $10\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 4</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.1)^{4}}=81962.$$

**Answer.** $₹81962$
